title: Garlicky Juicy Peruvian Epilogue Ceviche
description: A garlicky, juicy, nostalgic Peruvian dish featuring creamy epoisses and a tangy beurre-monte, perfect for a low-carb-friendly meal.
instructions: Cut the sea bass into small cubes.
In a large bowl, combine the sea bass, red onion, cilantro, and lime juice.
Mix well to combine, then season with salt to taste.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, stir in the softened epoisses cheese and minced garlic.
Top with whipped beurre-monte and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Serve chilled or at room temperature, depending on your preference.
Note: For an extra spicy kick, add a diced jalapeno pepper to the bowl with the sea bass.
Note: If you can't find epoisses cheese, you can substitute it with another strong, creamy cheese like goat cheese or mascarpone.
Note: Be sure to cook the beurre-monte just before serving, as it will thicken quickly in the refrigerator.
Note: If you're short on time, you can prepare the beurre-monte up to a day in advance and store it in the refrigerator until ready to use.
Note: For a lighter version of the dish, use only 1/8 cup of beurre-monte or substitute it with a mixture of sour cream and chives.
Note: This recipe makes 4-6 servings, depending on serving size.
Note: If you're not a fan of spicy food, omit the jalapeno pepper or substitute it with a milder pepper like Anaheim or bell pepper.
Note: You can also add diced mango or pineapple to the bowl with the sea bass for a sweet and tangy twist.
Note: To make the dish more substantial, add some cooked rice, quinoa, or grilled chicken to the bowl with the sea bass.
